title: taraxacum subguttulosum
intro: Taraxacum subguttulosum, also known as the lesser hawk's beard, is a type of dandelion-like plant that belongs to the sunflower family. It is often used in herbal medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, such as reducing swelling and promoting digestion, as well as its ability to support immune function and act as a natural diuretic. Taraxacum subguttulosum can be prepared in various herbal forms, including teas, infusions, and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety in human use.
